title: InfiniteLine3D Object
description: Transient 3D infinite line. An infinite line is defined by a position and direction in space and has no start or end points. They are created statically using the create method of the InfiniteLine3D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns a copy of this line object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the infinite line.

methods.name: intersectWithCurve
methods.description: Intersect this line with a curve to get the intersection point(s).

methods.name: intersectWithSurface
methods.description: Intersect this line with a surface to get the intersection point(s).

methods.name: isColinearTo
methods.description: Compare this line with another to check for collinearity.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the infinite line.

properties.name: curveType
properties.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

properties.name: direction
properties.description: Gets and sets the direction of the line.

properties.name: evaluator
properties.description: Returns an evaluator object that lets you perform additional evaluations on the curve.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Gets and sets the origin point of the line.

classType.title: InfiniteLine3D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: InfiniteLine3D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns a copy of this line object.
copy.return_description: Returns an independent copy of this line object.

create.title: InfiniteLine3D.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the new InfiniteLine3D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point of the line.

getData.title: InfiniteLine3D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the infinite line.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The output origin point of the line.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The output direction of the line.

intersectWithCurve.title: InfiniteLine3D.intersectWithCurve Method
intersectWithCurve.description: Intersect this line with a curve to get the intersection point(s).
intersectWithCurve.return_description: Returns a collection of the intersection points.

parameters.name: curve
parameters.description: The intersecting curve. The curve can be a Line3D, InfininteLine3D, Circle3D, Arc3D, EllipticalArc3D, Ellipse3D, or NurbsCurve3D.

intersectWithSurface.title: InfiniteLine3D.intersectWithSurface Method
intersectWithSurface.description: Intersect this line with a surface to get the intersection point(s).
intersectWithSurface.return_description: Returns a collection of the intersection points.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description: The intersecting surface. The surface can be a Plane, Cone, Cylinder, EllipticalCone, EllipticalCylinder, Sphere, Torus, or a NurbsSurface.

isColinearTo.title: InfiniteLine3D.isColinearTo Method
isColinearTo.description: Compare this line with another to check for collinearity.
isColinearTo.return_description: Returns true if the two lines are collinear.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The line to compare with for collinearity.

set.title: InfiniteLine3D.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the infinite line.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point of the line.

transformBy.title: InfiniteLine3D.transformBy Method
transformBy.return_description: Return true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the curve.

curveType.title: InfiniteLine3D.curveType Property
curveType.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = infiniteLine3D_var.curveType

direction.title: InfiniteLine3D.direction Property
direction.description: Gets and sets the direction of the line.

syntax.python:

# Set the value of the property.
infiniteLine3D_var.direction = propertyValue

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
